title: Orders — full workflow map

script.voiceover: Placing an order? Use the same five steps every time. Lab, med, imaging, referral — same shape.
One. Look at the top of the chart. Check the patient name and the encounter you're in. Wrong encounter is the number one reason orders land in the wrong place.
Two. In the chart sidebar, click Orders or Order Entry. If you don't see it, your role or department context is off — fix that first.
Three. Pick the order type, then search by name and select the exact orderable.
Four. Check the details panel. Priority, frequency, dose, indication. Anything starred or red is required before you can sign.
Five. Click Sign. If you're not the responsible provider, route for cosign instead.
If it worked, the order moves to Active and shows up on the order list.
If sign fails, read the inline message — it names the missing field. Fill it in and sign again.
Still stuck after two tries? Capture your role, the patient encounter, the order name, and the exact error text, then send it to your support contact.

title: Notes — where to write and sign

script.voiceover: Writing a note? Use the same five steps every time. Same shape as orders.
One. Check the patient name and the encounter at the top of the chart. The note will attach to whatever encounter is open right now.
Two. In the chart sidebar, click Notes or Documentation. If Notes is missing, your role or department context is off — fix that first.
Three. Pick the note type. Progress, H and P, procedure, discharge. The note type controls which template and required fields you'll see.
Four. Fill in every starred section. The editor will not let you sign until they're done.
Five. Click Sign. If a cosign is needed, route it to the right provider.
If it worked, the note moves to Signed and shows on the chart.
If sign fails, read the inline error — it names the exact missing field. Fill it in and try again.
Still stuck after two tries? Capture your role, the encounter, the note type, and the exact error text, then send it to your support contact.

title: SmartTools — SmartLink blank or wrong

script.voiceover: A SmartLink came up blank, or pulled the wrong info? Work it in four quick checks before you escalate.
One. Patient and encounter. Check the top of the chart. SmartLinks pull from the chart you're in — wrong chart, wrong data.
Two. Refresh the note, or delete the SmartLink and insert it again. A stale insert won't pick up new data until it re-resolves.
Three. Look for a prompt. Some SmartLinks need a date range, a result type, or a user pick before they return anything.
Four. If it's a shared template or shared SmartTool and it's still blank, this is a build issue, not a click issue. That means it needs the template owner.
If one of the first three checks fixed it, you're done.
If you reach check four, capture the SmartLink name, the patient encounter, and a screenshot, then route it to the documentation or template owner.
